mnemonics: PMF = P(x) = Probability of x occurring
PDF = p(x) = Probability density of x occurring
keyPoints: Probability mass function (PMF): assigns a probability to each possible outcome of a discrete random variable.
Probability density function (PDF): assigns a probability density to each possible outcome of a continuous random variable.
The sum of probabilities in a PMF is equal to 1 (P(X) = 1), while the integral of a PDF is equal to 1 (∫p(x)dx = 1).
